text:
Equipment: RIE

Sequence of RIE Gameplay

Enter the front-room of the RIE training room.

Check the tablet for video instructions on support-room RIE setup and perform the tasks demonstrated in the video:

Enter the support-room.

Go to the Nitrogen Tank and turn it on by rotating the valve to the left/clockwise. (Validate?)

Go to the Betta Tech Industrial Heater Chiller [1].

Turn on the “MAINS” rocker switch. The light directly above it should turn orange.

Turn on the “COOLING” rocker switch. The light directly above it should also turn orange.

Go to the Oxygen Tank [2]. and rotate the valve to the left/clockwise to turn it on ,and turn on other gases as needed.

Go to the front-room.

Go to the RIE Supply Valves [3].

Turn on the compressed air and nitrogen by rotating the handles 90 degrees until they are in-line with the brass fittings.

<OFF <ON

Check the tablet for video instructions on front-room RIE setup and perform the tasks demonstrated in the video:

Go to the RIE Machine [4].

Rotate the emergency “STOP” button clockwise to release it. The button should pop outwards upon release. See video for interactions.

Equipment: RIE

Open the door of the machine. This should remain open for the duration of operation.

Press the green “ON” button.

Go to the Small Chiller [5].

Turn it on by pressing the I/O button.

Wait until the displayed temperature gets down to 20 °C. Indicate to the user that this usually takes some time, roughly 5 minutes.

Check the tablet for the next video on how to evacuate and pressurize the RIE chamber and perform the tasks demonstrated in the video:

Go to the Computer.

Open PC 2000 from the desktop.

Show a login animation that automatically accepts all alerts (Manual steps 8 & 9)

Go to the RIE Machine.

Press the status button and make note of the ATP (turbo pump) and ACT (machine controller) temperature values. These should be closely monitored at all times. (These controls are inside the door of the RIE machine)

Return to the Computer.

Highlight and select “Evacuate”.

Highlight and select “Cancel” to indicate there is no wafer inside the chamber.

Go back to the RIE Machine and note the turbo pump speed value. Wait until it counts down to 27000 rpm.

Return to the Computer. After the correct rpm is reached it will say “Base pressure reached”.

Equipment: RIE

Highlight and select “Stop” to end the evacuation process.

Highlight and select “Venting”.

When the status reads “Finished” highlight and select “Stop”.

Check the tablet for the next video on how to load the sample/wafer and perform the tasks demonstrated in the video.

Go to the RIE Machine.

Turn the black knob to “CHAMBER UP”.

Press both light blue “HOIST” buttons simultaneously until the lid fully opens. It will rise up and rotate counterclockwise 90 degrees.

Put the sample on the stage.

Turn the knob back to “CHAMBER DOWN”.

Press both light blue “HOIST” buttons simultaneously until the lid reverses the same action and completely closes.

Return to the Computer.

Highlight and select “Evacuate”.

Highlight and select “OK” since a sample is now inside the RIE machine. Wait for the screen status to read “Base pressure reached”.

Go to the RIE Machine.

Turn on the “AC LINE” switch. The status light labeled “AC ON” should turn green. (These controls are inside the door of the RIE machine)

Check the tablet for the next video on how to etch and retrieve the sample and perform the tasks demonstrated in the video.

Return to the Computer.

Equipment: RIE

Highlight and select “Process”. A “Process Menu” dropdown will appear with the options “Recipes”, “Log View”, and “Chamber 1”.

Highlight and select “Recipes”.

Highlight and select “LOAD”.

From the list select the desired recipe name “XR Practice Etch”.

Highlight and select “OK”.

Highlight and select the recipe, then highlight and select “Edit Step”.

Reinforce the idea that the user should always double-check the flow rate, process time, and power numbers in any given recipe before using it.

Highlight and select “OK”.

Highlight and select “Run” to start running the recipe.

The status on the computer should read “All Tolerance OK”. Plasma should be visible in the black viewport that allows you to see into the RIE chamber.

Once the process is complete, accept all “Yellow Alerts” that pop up. Process Control should read “Ready” in green text.

Highlight and select “System”. Select “Pumping” from the dropdown.

Go to the RIE Machine.

Turn off the “AC LINE” switch. The green status light should turn off.

Return to the Computer.

Highlight and select “Stop”.

Highlight and select “Vent”.

Equipment: RIE

When the status text reads “Finished” highlight and select “Stop” to end the venting process.

Go to the RIE Machine.

Turn the black knob to “CHAMBER UP”.

Press both light blue “HOIST” buttons together to open the chamber lid.

Collect the sample.

Turn the black knob to “CHAMBER DOWN”.

Press both light blue “HOIST” buttons together to close the chamber lid.

Check the tablet for the closing procedures and perform the tasks demonstrated in the video.

Go to the Computer.

Highlight and select “Evacuate”.

Highlight and select “Cancel” since the wafer was retrieved from the stage. The chamber is now empty.

The computer status reads “Base pressure reached”.

Highlight and select “System”. From the dropdown highlight and select “Service”.

Select the “Turbo Pump” part of the animated RIE image in the software and highlight and select “YES” to turn it off.

Go to the RIE Machine. Demonstrate that the turbo pump speed is still dropping. Wait until it reaches 0 rpm.

Return to the Computer.

Highlight and select “System”. From the dropdown highlight and select “Exit Service”. Highlight and select “YES” to confirm that you want to stop using this part of the software.

Equipment: RIE

Highlight and select “System”. From the dropdown highlight and select “Exit System” to completely exit the software back to the desktop of the computer.

Go to the RIE Machine and press the red “OFF” button. Note that it will automatically turn off the small chiller in the front-room.

Press the red emergency “STOP” button inwards.

Go to the RIE Supply Valves.

Turn off the compressed air and nitrogen valves by rotating them to their original position.

Go to the support-room.

Go to the Oxygen Tank and rotate the valve to the right/counterclockwise to shut it off.

Go to the Betta Tech Industrial Heater Chiller.

Turn off the “COOLING” rocker switch. The light directly above it will go dim.

Turn off the “MAINS” rocker switch. The light directly above it will go dim.

Exit the support-room, and exit the front-room. End of RIE practice module.

Questions:

Should the user do anything with the nitrogen tank in the support-room?

Equipment: RIE

PC software navigation is a lot more intensive than the spin coater controls in the litho room. How do we want to go about providing a more involved guided user experience? Do we like the idea of highlighting the buttons they need to click/press instead of providing video instructions? >Only using video instructions would be really confusing...

Where is “All Tolerance OK” written in the software when running the recipe?

What shows up when you select “System” from the top toolbar. Does it have a dropdown like “Process”? If so what are the options besides “Pumping”?

Can a video be recorded of the software execution the next time a full dry etching process is conducted to ensure simulation accuracy?

How much time are we expecting to on the small chiller in the simulation, I hope not 5 mins :)

Step no 4(c), how many alerts should player accept and what are they?

Step no 4(c), what if player clicks other buttons other than accept? Do we want the player to click other than accept? If yes, what is the route taken from then?

Important Resources:

RIE Operation Manual

Equipment References:

[1] Betta Industrial Heater Chiller (Support-room)

[2] Oxygen Tank (Support-room)

Equipment: RIE

[3] RIE Shutoff Valves (Front-room) Shown in off position

[4] RIE Machine (Front-room)

[5] Small Chiller (Front-room)

Additional Equipment that was not indexed:

Required: Computer (Front-room)

In Question: Nitrogen Tank (Support-room), Freon/Other Etching Gases? (Support-room)
